name: Bagels 1
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine warm water, yeast, and sugar in a large bowl. Let stand for 5 minutes.
2. Stir in salt. Gradually mix in flour until a stiff dough forms.
3. Turn out on a floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic (about 15 minutes), adding more flour as needed. The dough should be firmer than most yeast breads.
4. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover loosely, and let rise until nearly doubled (about 40 minutes).
5. Knead the dough lightly, divide into 12 equal pieces.
6. To shape each piece, knead it into a smooth ball. Hold the ball with both hands and poke your thumbs through the center.
7. Shape the bagel like a doughnut, about 3 to 3 1/2 inches across.
8. Place shaped bagels on a lightly floured board and cover loosely. Let stand in a warm place for 20 minutes.
9. Bring 3 quarts of water and 1 tbsp sugar to a boil in a large kettle.
10. Adjust the heat to keep the mixture boiling gently. Lightly grease a baking sheet and sprinkle with cornmeal.
11. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
12. Gently lift one bagel at a time from the boiling water and place it on the prepared baking sheet, turning occasionally for about 4 minutes.
13. Remove with a slotted spatula and drain briefly on a towel.
14. Brush each bagel lightly with an egg yolk and water glaze.
15. Bake for 35-40 minutes, or until well browned and crusty. Cool on a rack.
WHOLE WHEAT BAGELS: Follow the basic recipe, omitting sugar. Replace sugar with 3 tbsp honey. In place of flour, blend 2 cups whole wheat flour, 1/2 cup wheat germ, and about 2 3/4 cups all-purpose flour. Knead with white flour.
PUMPERNICKEL BAGELS: Follow the basic recipe, omitting sugar. Replace sugar with 3 tbsp dark molasses. In place of flour, use a blend of 2 cups rye, 2 cups whole wheat, and 1 3/4 cups all-purpose flour. Knead with white flour.
ONION BAGELS: Add 1/2 cup instant toasted onion to the dough along with yeast, water, and sugar in the basic recipe. SEEDED BAGELS: Sprinkle boiled bagels with sesame seeds, poppy seeds, or caraway seeds after brushing with egg wash and before baking.
